lint.note: package.json does not define a lint script, so the milestone cannot satisfy the expected test/typecheck/lint validator set as written.

blockingIssues.description: step1.txt contains hello with a trailing newline (`hello\n`) because the worker used plain `echo`, so VAL-FILE-001 is not satisfied.
The runtime behavior is correct, but the handoff records unrelated commit 598d18e as the feature commit after EndFeatureRun rejected the lack of a real commit, breaking the scrutiny evidence trail.
step3.txt contains a trailing newline (`done\n`) and the handoff also attributes the work to unrelated commit 598d18e, so both content correctness and provenance are invalid.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update the file-writer skill to prefer newline-safe writes such as `printf %s` or another exact-content method instead of plain `echo` when missions assert exact file contents.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: write-step1 and write-step3 followed the current pattern and produced `hello\n` / `done\n`, violating VAL-FILE-001 and VAL-FILE-003.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Define an explicit procedure for non-repo tasks that intentionally make no repository changes so workers are not forced to attach unrelated commit IDs to satisfy EndFeatureRun success requirements.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: write-step2 and write-step3 both used commit 598d18e even though their work only touched /tmp, and scrutiny reviewers flagged the provenance mismatch as blocking.

rejectedObservations.observation: Shared-state inconsistency around repo-local versus home-directory skill/services locations.
rejectedObservations.reason: ambiguous; reviewers observed path mismatches in transcripts, but there is not enough evidence in this run to determine the correct systemic fix.
